writing.title: To Santa's Rescue!
writing.text:
"Have you ever been really really excited about something"? "I was, because Christmas was tomorrow"!! "By the way I am 12 yrs old and I have a younger sister who's 8". "Goodnight honey", mom said. "Night mom". "Sophie wake up wake up you have to see this"!. "What, why are you in my room"? I said sleepily it was 11 am. What I saw made me absolutely breathtaken, a vibrant green light was coming out of my closet and the light was blinding. I felt a great source of pressure as if something was clenching me into the light. In a matter of seconds, I was laying on pure white snow and it was snowing on top of me.

I was shivering. " Katie, are you here"? I said "scared and cold". "Katie"? "Where are you"? "I am here"! "She also was scared"! " Where are we"? I pulled myself up, dusting the snowy powder of my pajamas. "Check this out, the snow when you walk on it doesn't turn disgusting it stays milky white.", Katie said.  "Cool", but we have to go back home, there has to be a way"! "What do you mean, we have to go back home"? "This is a different world". "What if Santa Claus lives here"?" "Stop being"-

Suddenly a tall lady in a blue gown appeared, she was very pretty. "What are you guys doing out here in the cold, come up to the castle, we can have hot chocolate together, my name is  Queen Miranda Davanda, what are your names"? . "It's very kind of you, my name is Sophie and my sister's name is Katie, but we are fine thanks". "No no you must be cold come up to the castle with me I insist". "Yeah Sophie, come on"! " Please excuse us for a minute", I told the lady. "BUT WE CAN"T TRUST A RANDOM LADY IT'IS NOT SAFE"!" But this is so cool please"???? "We get to go to a castle, and there's magic maybe"!!!!

"Fine, I grunted, and I promise there will be no magic"! "So are you coming"?, the lady in the beautiful gown asked. "Yes we are", I replied in some sort of a glum way. The castle was so pretty from the outside it was magical. It felt like we were in some sort of a  fantasy story. When we went inside it was enormous. There were so many rooms, so much to see, and just there were infinite corridors." It was the most mystical place I have ever been to". "I know right"? "This is so cool", Katie exclaimed. We entered a beautiful room. "Have a seat". "Thank you".

2 fairies handed us some milky sweet tea and sugar cookies, "oh my god this is so cool right Katie"? "I know right"?. "I called you here, you are in a magical world named Zambia" . "Listen, Santa's in trouble, he's kidnapped by the wicked witch of the west, each year, as you know Santa delivers presents to kids all over the world. If no one does anything, kids will be sad and start getting cranky". "If they don't get their presents by 6 am in the morning , you don't have much time to save santa but you still can, are you in for it"?

"Yes we can help you", Katie replied really excitedly. " We are sorry but we can't". "We should really get going home because mom would be very worried if we aren't in our beds by 7 am, I replied abruptly". "Then I guess no kids will get their presents on Christmas and everyone would start thinking that Santa's not real and that it's just fantasy".

"Please Sophie, if nobody gets their presents on Christmas kids and everyone will start getting cranky and will stop thinking that Santa's real, come on please for me"? "Ok fine, you're right everyone will be sad and lose the christmas spirit, we are in for it"!

"Thank you girls, now let me tell you what you will have to do, you will have to go to the Wicked Witch of the West's castle". "It is located in the far west in a small place named Dumba" . "Here's a map that will tell you what to do exactly". "And you have to reach back home at 5:45 so when your mom comes to check on you you will be safe". "After you have saved Santa, and helped him deliver all the presents". Santa will make sure you reach home by 5:45 am". Understand girls?. "Yesl", both of us exclaimed.

"Then we better get going". "Bye girls have a safe journey", "oh here these are some really warm coats you can wear there it is ice cold in Dumba". "Remember to be careful around the Wicked witch and this map explains once you reach Zambia what you should do to not get caught, that will tell you exactly what to do ok understand"?

"Ok thank you so much Queen, see you"!" Bye good luck girls, you got this".

"Wait wait, come here I have to give you this". "We both flipped our backs quickly". "What"? "Here, this is a magical watch that will let you know what time it is in this world okay"? "And this shall tell you when you have to go to the wicked witch which is written on the map, got it"?

"Yes, I got it thank you, see you"!

"First the map says we have to go to the magical train station-
"Cool",Katie interrupted. I know it's so cool, "so as I was saying the magical train station is straight after we go left and we should reach there in 2 min". "Ok, we are here"! "It's on platform  B oh that's over there". "Come on fast, the train's about to leave, we are late"!

"Oh my god the train doesn't have train tracks- W'cth your names, young lady's the train flyer said in a sort of a funny way. " I am Sophie and she is Katie, I said". "Nce names you got". Thank you I replied., The flyer was in a baggy orange shirt and baggy jeans, he had a tall face and curved and pointed chin". The train burst up in the air. I could feel the cool night air on my cheek. It was like flying on a magic mat!

"We reached Dumba in less than a minute", Katie said after getting out of the flying train. "Yeah we reach so quickly". Dumba looked like an exciting/unique place. It had so much going on, the hustle and bustle of Christmas, the popping  Christmas lights everywhere it was the most majestic place ever" .  And it was snowing so hard on us and we were very cold, but thanks to the Queen we had jackets so we fortunately didn't  turn into icicles. The Queen was right, it is extremely cold in Dumba.

"What does the map say to do next? Katie asked, feeling really excited and joyful. "It says that when it is pitch black around 10 pm- what time is it right now, it is 9:15 pm according to Dumba and Zambia time I have no idea what time it is back home though we have to do this fast we also have to help santa." "Ok so as I was saying the wicked witch sleeps by 10 so then we will go to her and try to save santa ok"? "Got it", Katie said in a nervous but excited voice.

"But what should we do right now because we have about 15 minutes"? "Do you just want to explore Dumba a bit"? "We don't really know this world but we can explore it a bit, let's do it".

Exploring Dumba was so cool. I loved the vibrant Christmas lights and we were having a really good time but now we had to go to the Wicked Witch of the West to save Santa. I was so nervous and we both were telling each other that everything is going to be alright.

"Ok we have to go now"! "Ok wait, the queen said her castle is west of Dumba, and on the map it says to go there as well, ok come with me". "Come on come on, I know you're tired of walking but we're almost there".

"We are here"! "OMG"! The Wicked Witch's castle was incredibly tall, it looked very creepy, it was painted pitch black and there were no lights. It was like something I had never seen in my life. "This is so scary and cool"! "I know right"?

"Sophie, what time is it?" Katie asked kind of nervously. "It is 9:57 come on, we have to save Santa before 10 pm!! "Or else the wicked witch will wake up"!

"Come on lets go wait this door is locked, wait let's try the back door". "Good idea". Yes this door works just hang around with me I don't want you getting lost understanding"? Okay, I get it!" "Okay good, come around with me here to this corridor."

"This is like a maze. It's so confusing right"? "Yes I know okay the map say's Santa is trapped in lock number 7, so that would be straight up the stairs the map says". "Ok come on we don't have much time it's 9:58 the Wicked Witch could wake up any second now"! "The stairs                                                                  are so creaky"! "Oh wait, don't make a  single peep when I see her. I saw her as well." She was in her bed and her bed looked nasty and green. "Ok be very careful don't say anything to me and don't make a single squeak get it"? "Got it". We climbed up the creaky stairs very slowly. It was so scary to climb up on the creaky stairs.

"Ok we're here oh wait I see santa I see santa shhhhh shhhhhh, don't talk loudly" I whispered instantly, terrified  ……. "WHAT DO I HEAR!"! A nasty growling voice said instantly. "Oh my god she has woken up ok wait let's hide in the corner here come quick". "YOU DON'T WANT TO HIDE FROM ME COME OUT THIS SECOND OR I WILL TURN YOU INTO SHRIMP CHUNKS"!!! "HAHAHAHA!"!!!

My heart was drumming against my chest. "Come on fast and quietly we have to hide in the corner fast come on". "Ok now be quiet or else we will get caught", I muttered silently.

"Hmmm, I must have heard something in my dreams". "There's no one out here I checked everywhere". "Stupid me", the wicked witch of the west said with what looked like a little bit of sadness.

"Few, that was so close". Shhhh, ok come on I see santa he's in that locker over there come on fast"!!! "Ok ok. Oh there he is".

"Thank you so much, Santa exclaimed, did Queen Miranda send you"? This is sooooo cool, yes she sent us"." Are you actually the real Santa"? "Of course I am"! "Can you say ho ho ho"? "I don't like to fake it". "Please please please please can you", we chanted? Fine…. HO HO HO. "That's so cool"! "Come on girls, we gotta get out of here, or else we'll get caught"!

We zoomed out of the castle as quickly as we could. We had freed Santa, I was so happy everybody will be happy tonight now, after we deliver their presents! And no one will be sad!

"Ok so as a thank you so much for you girls to save me, "do you want to help me deliver all the presents tonight"? "That would be aaaaamazing"! "We need to be back in our beds by 6 am though because mom comes to check on us at 6 am, will you be able to send us back by 5:45"? "Of course, we will be done by that time but we gotta hurry because we don't have a lot of time"!

In a matter of a few seconds with some sparks and magical dust, we reached Santa's toy shop. "OMG this is the coolest thing I have ever seen". It was warm and cozy, there were elves speaking elvish and there were millions and millions of gifts and letters here. It was unlike any place I have ever been. "Wow I love this place", Katie said excitedly. "I know, right"? "It's truly magical".

"OK guys, now let's go deliver presents, because we won't reach everyone in time if we don't leave now". "OK, come on Katie", I said. We sat on Santa's sleigh. It was very comfortable, followed by all of Santa's reindeers, Rudolf's big red nose was glowing in the darkness. "Ok girls ready to blow off"? YES, we both replied at the same time.

We could feel the chilly night air freshening our faces, as we were flying in the night sky. It was unlike anything we have ever done in our lives. It was a magical experience, "having fun"? Santa asked, with a little bit of funny mischief in his scruffy voice. "Yes, we love it"! We both replied enthusiastically.

"Guys according to my fob watch if we don't deliver all the presents by 5 am, people will start getting cranky and angry, so we gotta hurry this up it's already 2 am, we only have 3 more hrs"! Santa said with some panic in his voice.

"Don't worry Santa we got this". "OK, we are here in the first neighborhood. I will go through the chimney and you girls throw the presents down to me each time I will keep telling you the names, ok"? OK. "Hannah", here you go. "James", here. Sophia, I tossed it. Sam, here. And then soon we went to the next neighborhood and the next and so on.

"We did it"! Santa seemed very happy, "thanks girls"! "Of course any time". We came to Santa's house. It was very warm and cozy with red walls. We also met Ms. Clause, she was wearing a tight bun on her head and a warm fur coat, she also had a very sweet smile.

"Hello, it's so cool to meet you", I said. "Thank you honey, welcome to our house". "Oh my, you look so cold"! "Would you like hot chocolate"? "Yes please". Ms. Clause handed over steaming creamy mugs of hot chocolate. After we were done drinking it was 5:40 am so me and Katie hugged Santa and Ms. Clause and said thank you and goodbye and then with a dazzle of magical santa dust we found ourselves back in our beds.

We had the best time of our lives. We decided to keep it a secret though and to not tell anyone about the epic adventure because everyone would think it's not true. I was very sleepy and Katie too, as soon as I touched my pillow I slept instantly. "I can't wait to go on another adventure like that again", Katie said, "me too"!

The end.

writing.title: Is It Better to Be a Good Person… or to Be Seen as a Good Person?
writing.text:
"Is it better to be a good person… or to be seen as a good person?"

It is better to truly be a good person than when people think that someone is a good person when that person is not. Here are some reasons why it's better to be a good person from the heart: because the person is nice and they actually care or when your feelings are hurt you can tell them, and someone who is always there for you no matter what you do or no matter what happens.

Being a good person doesn't mean to be perfect, you can still get angry sometimes, sad or nervous. But being a good person just means that someone thinks for everyone truly from the bottom of their heart, and for example if someone is alone in school at lunchtime or recess, ask them to join you, it's more fun with more people and they also get company!

If you are actually a good person people will start seeing that, people will like you, you will make friends, you will be trustworthy to other people, people will want to be near you, and most people will like you. But if you are not actually a good person and just act like you are then people will soon see your real personality and people wouldn't want to be near you.

I could be wrong and being seen as a good person could be better. But I personally think that being seen as a good person doesn't actually make you a good person, because you are just faking it and soon people will realize it. But who knows I can be wrong.

Now some people might argue that if "You are seen as good then what's the point of actually being good"? But I don't believe that because being seen as good will only be temporary before people actually get to know you. And once they know your personality no one would want to talk or hang out with you.

Another reason why I don't believe that is because I am a good person I care deeply about people I always be nice, because I have been bullied before in school and I know how it feels, I know how it feels to be left out, I know how it feels to people not liking you in a mean way and not thinking that you are cool, and they just think they are cool. Being bullied is one of the worst feelings anyone could ever receive and it feels oddly bad. It feels like you are not special.

But being bullied teached me to not let people hurt me mentally, and to always include people who are very lonely or look very lonely, because just think if that happened to you. How would you feel? Sad, right? So everyone feels that way, everyone has feelings, everyone has the right to have friends and talk to people.

I hope you understand why being a good person is better than being seen as a good person, and how people will like you, how they will talk to you, how they will think of you and how to handle big emotions.

writing.title: Why Is It Easier to Remember Stories Than Facts?
writing.text:
"Why is it easier to remember stories than facts"?

This is an amazing question. Here's what I think about it, firstly stories have a hook, they have a whole story either made up or real it's made on. And when you keep reading and keep reading and you finally finish the book. If you pay attention to the book, you will remember what the characters did, you will know what the story was about, and you will also be able to appreciate the story in a much better and more satisfying way in an emotional way. Facts are small ideas or words and there are so many of them worldwide so it is very hard to keep track of which fact was which, and how every fact actually makes sense. But with stories there are a lot of pages on 1 big idea which helps you concentrate better on what is actually going on and how it's happening. Authors have to put in insane amounts of effort into making books creative, fun, engaging, imaginative and memorable. It takes a lot of time, concentration and determination to make extremely long really memorable books that always stay in your life like a little gift and you will always have that small amazing exciting feeling in you that magic exists, but humans haven't discovered it yet.

Now amazing authors like J.K Rowling and Rick Roirden are super popular today, because of the worlds they created, of how kids actually felt like they were in those amazing worlds that is what actually made their stories memorable and unforgettable. They had to make their books perfect in all areas. Their characters were really strong and kind like Harry, Hermione, Percy and Annabeth. It felt like they were real kids fighting for what's right, and being brave in scary or sad situations. Their kind and brave traits also made them feel real. Stories also create twists and lots of emotional moments which is why they stick in your brain forever like glue and you can never forget those extremely heartfelt moments. Because those moments actually made you feel like you are the one who was very close to the person. For example when Sirius died, I cried because that attachment was just so so deep. Sirius was such a kind and unforgettable character from Harry Potter who was always there for Harry whenever he could be. He was the only family he had left after his mom and dad died. He was his amazing godfather. The brain is so dependent on what we feel and when we lose the thing it's just extremely sad. That is also what makes some rare stories actually something you remember and actually something you appreciate and learn from every single day.

Now, you might be wondering: "How did these authors actually think of this stuff"?, "how did they actually make their stories feel so real and hearttouching"? Well the answer to that is that they as kids read so many books like thousands and thousands of books, which made them think so widely imaginatively and become who they have become today. They also wrote as a kid, wrote about 30% of the time and read about 70% of the time. Reading is more important as it is the thing that actually makes them think to make all of the plots, twists and characters, but writing allows them to write down their creative ideas on paper to actually start writing a magnificent story with unforgettable times, and characters that you today will remember as a reader. It is not easy to make long books like Harry Potter or Percy Jackson, but with time and with lots and lots of encouragement, lots of tries and with determination they did it. Books not only are great for us but they also make us a totally different person in how we think, what we do, how we react to situations and what kind of person we have become today and how to actually appreciate life.

writing.title: Come or Lose: The 3 Day Countdown
writing.text:
"Have you ever been really really upset about something"? Well I was, because me and my family were moving houses. I would have to leave all my friends, leave the school that I love, and move into some boring old house! "Hazel come down, we are leaving"! My mom yelled from the living room. "Coming", I said quickly.

I rushed down the stairs. My little 10 yr old brother Ollie was also frustrated that we were moving. "You guys packed everything"? "Yes, yes, I replied glumly". 'Are you sure you got your phone and everything Hazel"?

"Yes"! "Ok then lets go"! It was a very chilly glum day, the wind almost made me fall over. I loved my city where I lived, New York, it was my place where I grew up from 10 yrs, and now I am moving. Tears were streaming down my cheeks. I couldn't help it. Even though I was usually brave and I was 15 I sometimes just can't help it. Everything I loved was slowly getting out of the view. I felt like my life was over.

I was leaving all my friends, my amazing school, my house, everything just went in a flash. We were moving to a totally different place, who knows what might be there? What if I don't make any friends? What if people there were mean and unfriendly? Just shut it, I told myself.

"Look, Hazel, Ollie, I know this move is hard for you guys". "I understand that". "But trust me this will work out and you are going to love New Orleans"! "You will make amazing new friends and you will love our new house". Dad just didn't understand how I felt right right now. The tears wouldn't stop, my sea-green eyes were all puffy and swollen from all that crying. Then after an hour I finally told myself that get a grip and that it will be ok.

 The rest of the way to the city was terrible. I was feeling horrible. I stared out the window. It was a gray and dull evening. The car ride was very long, 10 hrs, and only 1 hr went so far. It felt like hours were gone already. Then finally we reached.

The house wasn't like any ordinary house. It was very old and antique looking. It was enormous, it looked like a killer's house. I didn't like the vibe of the house at all, it felt like something was wrong. "Isn't this house great"? Mom said, trying to break the fact that the house was so weird. "I hate this place", I muttered under my breath. "Oh come on honey, I know this is a new place for you and Ollie but it will all be good, ok"?

 "Come on, let's go check out our new house! "Fine", I replied glumly. The house was massive, there were cobwebs hanging from the walls and some paint was peeling. There were 3 rooms and the kitchen was musty. Plus it smelled funny. "We just need a bit of re-furnishing", dad said. "I agree", mom said.

"This will be your new room Hazel", the room was spacious but it was very dirty. "Don't worry we'll get that all cleaned up soon". And Ollie, this is your room, it was a bedroom that looked extremely creepy and it was very dusty as well. "We will get that all cleaned up as well", don't worry, my dad said reassuringly to Ollie as he gave them the "what is this" look. I had my arms crossed, this house was worse than I expected, I was missing home already. New Orleans was a very very rainy place. "Why don't you and Ollie go outside for a small walk to see how New Orleans is", mom said?

Ugh, fine I finally said. The breeze brushed through my long wavy chocolate brown hair. It was cloudy and gloomy. "Oh well hello there"! I got jumpscared so badly I almost banged my head on the brick wall. "Oh hi sorry I got scared for a moment". "Oh not to worry about that". I live right next door to you. Our new neighbor had long dark black hair, she was unusually skinny and she was wearing a black wavy dress with black high heels.

 "Did you guys just move in"? She said it in a voice that was mischievous, as if she was trying to build up on something big. "Um yea, we moved in like an hour ago". She kept whispering strangely about something. What are your names?

"I am Hazel and he is Ollie, my little brother." "What's your name"? Well I am sure you will hate this neighborhood because of its… Well good day to you, see you around. "Hey come back, would you mean because of it"? "And what's your name?" more, before I knew it she was gone. Did I just imagine it or did she just vanish in smoke? I told myself stop making things up, you are gonna scare Ollie. "Come on Ollie, let's go, we are going back home".

Just as we were going home, a beautiful lady appeared. She was wearing a light blue gown. She had fair skin. She wore huge gold hoops in her ears. Her eyes were as green as emeralds. And she wore a tiara over her beautiful bun. She looked extremely kind.

"Honey this is the sword of freedom it can kill anyone no matter who they are". "Use it only in times of extreme need". "You can use it only once". "You will need it". She disappeared. "Wait, come back"! She didn't come back. That was also strange, I thought to myself. But something told me that the lady was right. I will need it.

We went back to our old dusty new home. Again it had that same something that was off feeling. Mom was preparing dinner in the kitchen. "Come on guys lets eat". "Bon apettit", dad said! Mom and dad were clearly trying to make things look fun and cool, but nothing was making me feel any better. First we moved houses, then that mischievous lady, then the sword appeared. What was all this? Was it some sort of prank or dream?

I told myself that this is all actually happening. "Mom, when me and Ollie went for a walk there was this strange lady who was introducing herself as our neighbor, and I told her sudden appearance, how she left that sentence for us to kind of figure out and how she seemed to vanish in a cloud of smoke. I thought it would be best to not tell her about the sword."Haha, the vanish part wasn't real honey".

"It's probably nothing". "It's probably a nice lady trying to get to know you guys and be friendly"."No, but mom there was something weird about that lady as if she was hiding something from us". "Nothing is wrong, this is a very safe place, everything is fine". "Got it"? "Mhm", I nodded with pizza in my mouth, although I didn't mean it.

"You are starting a new school from tomorrow are you both excited"? "No", both of us exclaimed! "Well I am sure you will like it". Me and Ollie didn't say anything. We had an odd moment of silence. I cut up my chicken and stuffed buttery mashed potatoes in my mouth. I loved mashed potatoes, they were a comfort food, and reminded me of my home city New York.

After dinner, I went to bed. It was already 10 and I had school tomorrow. Mom came and kissed me good night. "Everything will be fine, don't worry Hazel". I nodded. "Good night mom", I said. Then she left me and I was all alone in my dark glum room.

 I tried to sleep but I couldn't. I tossed and turned, but nothing seemed to work. Then finally I fell asleep. I was having an extremely fun dream, where I was back in New York with all my friends and we were hanging out.

Then suddenly at exactly 3:17 am I heard the doorbell ring like 10 times. I got so scared I ran to my parents room with all my energy. "Mom dad who's that"? But what I looked at was the most frightening sight ever. My mom and dad were dead and Ollie was gone.

 There was no sight of him. There was a note right next to him that said good luck finding your brother Hazel, it said you have 3 days to find your brother or else we will kill him. He was kidnapped. There was no sign of anyone.

I burst into tears. I cried so terribly my heart was beating like a roller coaster, my hands were cold, clammy and sweaty,  my eyes were puffy and red, I had never been this sad before. "What was I going to do"? I had lost my city, I lost my friends, I lost my parents and I lost my brother"? I cried and cried this was not possible. No one I knew was here.

 I was all alone. Did that mysterious lady do this? All I knew was that whoever did all of this to my family is going to be dead. And I will kill them. And I will find my brother no matter what.

Even if that means that I get killed. All I cared about right now was my family. I would do anything to get them back. But my mom and dad were gone for life, they were dead, and if I don't find my brother in 3 days he would be dead too, but I won't let that happen. I had lost enough. One part of my family is not gonna be dead Ollie, he's gonna come back alive.

My family was all I had left and now they were gone? I lost control over myself. I had never cried like this in my life. I had never felt like this in my life. Who did this, I demanded to myself. I told myself it was all my fault. My heart felt like it was shattering to pieces, this was unlike something I had never experienced before. I searched for any other clues but I found nothing. There was no help. I had to do this all by myself. My mind couldn't process this. Life now felt impossible.

Then finally it was morning. The day was dull, gray and dark again rain was slashing the windowsills. I had to find Ollie in 3 days. But the problem was I had no clue at all where he was. I couldn't go to school, since I had to find him by a deadline. I read the note again from last night. Good luck finding your brother Hazel, you have 3 days to find him or he's DEAD. First of all, how did this person know my name? I slept very uncomfortably and miserably.

The next day was very rainy and chilly. The only person who knew my name in my new neighborhood was that lady. OMG, that means she was the one who did all of this. I wore my black fluffy coat and went next door. Since she said she was my neighbor. I knocked on the door nervously. I bit my lip. An old man opened the door, "oh hello there"! He looked very friendly. Oh yea, sorry, bye. That was awkward, I sighed.

That night when I went to bed, I had lost all hope but I wasn't gonna give up. It took me hours to fall asleep. Then after 3hrs I finally did. The next morning I woke up to an unpleasant cloudy/dark day again. This place had no sun. Then an idea struck me, I thought that I should go to my friendly neighbor and see if he knew anything. "I had lost all hope, but I wasn't gonna quit". I was scared today was the last day left, if I didn't find Ollie today it was done.

I put on my black winter coat, hat and mittens. Then I went outside his house, and rang the doorbell. "Oh well hello again", he exclaimed! "Hi", I said! "Mind if I come inside"? "I need to discuss something"?

 "Oh sure". "Just a moment, wait here I need to do something". I waited, it was strange how he told me to wait outside as if he was plotting something. I shook my head and told myself I was making stuff up, this man was very nice. "Come in", he said. His house was small, crooked and it looked pretty old.

"Take a seat", he said warmly. "Want some tea"? "Um, sure," I said. I was very cold. "Thanks", I said. The tea warmed me up. It was comforting and sweet. "So what is it you wanted to talk about"?" I know this might sound crazy", I said. I told him everything about how someone killed my parents and kidnapped Ollie and that note.

"I am so sorry", he said! I tried to not cry but tears came running down my cheeks. "It's all gonna be alright", he said. Then suddenly just when I was about to leave I heard a high pitched screaming voice. "What's that noise? I demanded.

"Oh honey, that's probably just the neighbors, they have a lot of fights". Don't worry about that. Then again just when I was about to leave I heard the same voice coming from the old man's attic. "WHAT IS IT"?! "Look honey, it's just the neighbors fighting nothing else, but his face was twitching. I could tell something was wrong. "Before he could stop me I flooded into the basement". "I couldn't believe my eyes at what I saw".

"Ollie"!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! He was bleeding from every part, he looked like he was about to die.The friendly looking man was in a disguise, when he took it off it was no longer a friendly old man, but he was the strange lady we met! "YOU"! I lunged towards him. "You killed my parents and you kidnapped my brother"!!!!!!!!

WHY?!!!!! Why did you do this? I demanded. I got the knife out of my bag, which I kept for emergencies. My big gold hoops dangling over my ears. I got the massive foldable knife out of my bag quickly and she said. "Not so fast kid". HAHAHAHAHA HAHAHAHAHA, the lady cackled. She was strangling Ollie.

 "NOOOOOOOOOOOO"! I ran as fast as I could, and I pushed the lady backwards. She could do magic. She was a witch. "You can't kill me, you can't kill me"! "There's got to be a way", I thought.

Then it clicked in my mind the sword of freedom. I checked everywhere it wasn't in my bag or anywhere. Ollie I will be back in 1 minute. This is an emergency. I have to go to the house to get something, just a moment. Don't worry I will be back. I ran like lightning, I had no time to waste. I took the keys and unlocked my house.

 I searched for the sword but it was nowhere to be found. Then finally I found it in my bedroom closet. When I went back to save Ollie the door was locked. I could hear Ollie screaming. Think I told myself, think. I couldn't use the sword more than once.

Then an idea struck me. I ran to the witch's kitchen cabinet, she probably had a potion to open things with. I went to the kitchen cabinet and searched everywhere. But it was nowhere to be seen. Then the beautiful lady who had given me the sword suddenly appeared again. "You have been a very brave child". She opened the door for me and left immediately. Before I could say thank you. I had no time to waste.

I rushed downstairs. YOU, how did you manage to come back the witch said!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Before she could do anything I took the sword of freedom out of my bag and stabbed it in her chest. Before I knew it she was dead and only a cloud of dead smoke remained.

I ran to Ollie. I was crying. "Ollie I am so sorry all this happened to you". "You did it little bro". He gave me a bone cracking hug. "You came for me Hazel"! "Of course I would"! We were both crying. He told me all about what had happened, how the witch made him do everything for her, how the witch threatened him that he would die if he didn't do it. And how he was getting no food. And how he cried every single night.

"I am so sorry to hear all that but now everything's good"? "Right"? "What happened to mom and dad"? "Where are they"? I couldn't get myself to reveal the truth but I knew I had to. "Ollie, mum and dad are dead". I pulled back my tears. I couldn't afford to cry in front of Ollie. I am sorry. Ollie burst into tears. There was nothing that could be done now. After about an hour of him crying nonstop, he finally stopped. Ollie, it's going to be ok, okay? He nodded, trying to pull himself together.

After a few months eventually me and Ollie started going to school, we made friends and it was all better now. My best friend was Olivia. She was always very kind and supportive. Whenever I needed help she always helped me. I took care of Ollie everyday. I was the mom of the house now.

Then one day unexpectedly the beautiful lady came again. "Oh hi"!!! "What a pleasure to meet you again", I said. "Thank you so much for all the help that day"! "Don't worry about it," she said, smiling. "Mind if I join you for dinner"? "By the way what's your name"? Sophia. "Of course you can join us"! I said. I made creamy garlicky mashed potatoes with crispy chicken and peas. "Mmmmm I love this"! She said, "happy you liked it"!

We told her everything and what had happened. How did you know about all this? Because I am special I know everything that's happening everywhere, my job is to help people. "Oh that explains it", I said. After dinner she said, "ok I have to go now to help someone else thanks for having me"! "Also here this is my phone number anytime you need help or are in a dangerous situation, I am happy to help".

"Thank you, I said"! From that day on we lived happily. With Sophia visiting us, having someone kind, and having friends. New Orleans wasn't as bad after all Ollie also made friends, he also started liking New Orleans. Even though life sometimes felt hard and tough after mom and dad died , I tried my best to be the best sister to Ollie and to always keep him happy.

The End.

writing.title: 20 Skills That Will Matter Most in the Future
writing.text:
"What are the 20 skills that will matter most in the future, and how can I start learning them now?"

1. Math. Not only will math make me smarter as a human when I grow up, but it will help me a lot to get into good universities and to get a good job. I can start learning this now by doing it mostly everyday for 30-60 min, (which I already do).

2. Writing. Writing literally decides how you think or communicate. To understand emotions, feelings, and be more social or even have creative ideas you need writing as a skill. I can do this by writing for 15-20 min daily (I don't do it daily (I used to), which I should start doing again).

3. Leadership. Leadership makes you get along with people better. It shows that you can lead people your way. It enriches confidence, and it also makes you more independent. I can learn leadership by going to a camp or going to an activity and learning to lead it. Or even just make up the mindset of being a leader.

4. Courage. Courage is something that you really need. If you have courage you can stand up for people, you can do something that's unusually brave, something that you're nervous or shy about, but you end up doing anyway. I can do this by doing something that might not always feel the most comfortable, standing up for a friend, or doing something extremely brave that was very hard to do. I am ok at this but not that good at this yet.

5. Communication. This literally is one of the most important skills, knowing how to communicate with people and what to say and what not to. Communication is the key to having good friends, good relationships, good experiences and so much more. I can do this by being a good and kind friend (which I already do) and by writing also.

6. Having emotional intelligence is also a huge one. Emotional intelligence means understanding people's emotions and being supportive and kind. It also means to help people whenever they need it. And it also means building people's trust. Emotional intelligence can literally decide whether people like you as a person or not. I can do this by understanding people, and supporting people or a friend when they need it (I already do this).

7. Self Discipline is also a big one. Self Discipline doesn't mean never do anything, it just means to be able to do something without being told and being responsible for it. It matters because it teaches responsibility and healthy habits. This I think is the strongest one I do here's why: I aim for 2 hrs deep study daily and end up doing it on most days, I have done a few huge reading days and showed that I can end a book even if its 200-300 pages away, I write my journal every day (sometimes I miss it but write what I did on the missed day the day after), and I have been maintaining that journal for over 1 year or 3 months, and I just finished it about a week ago and started a new one. I feel like I have maintained this habit for an extremely long time and it helps me a LOT.

8. Being able to focus is also a big one. Being able to focus literally determines if you can do well on tests, studying, sports, activities, school and so much more. I can do this by focusing during school, and my studying time (I already do this, I focus in class and during studying and reading). I am not saying you have to be focused every single minute of your life just through important stuff but taking breaks is very important too.

9. Time management is another big one. Being able to be flexible, if you have a test coming tomorrow and you have sleepover the day before, you have to learn to do your sleepover some other day since you should study for your test. I feel like I am not that good at this.

10. Having a hobby is a really big one as well. You have to have a passion for something you enjoy doing whether it's drawing, reading, making a puzzle, something you like doing in your free time or for fun. I currently have 2, drawing and reading. I have small miner ones too. I can do this by spending time on my hobbies.

11. Being a good and fun friend is also a really big one. When you grow up you mostly live with friends, in universities, and so much more, friends become a MAJOR part of your life. With close friends, you can laugh, hang out and share stuff too. I think I am a good and fun friend.

12. Being able to work for long hours is also a really important habit. You have to have this habit because sometimes you need to study for hours for a test, or for applying for a job, for the job you go to, or preparing for a competition. There can be so many different reasons why you may have to work hard. I can do this by doing what I do already, studying for 2 hrs mostly daily.

13. Consistency is one of the most important skills yet. Not only does it determine how good you get at something you love to do, but it also determines what type of person you become in the future. I feel like I am really good at this since I study daily for 2 hrs mostly.

14. Having grit. Grit is one of the hardest skills you can have at a young age. It's one of those skills which includes having LOTS of patience. Grit means to stick with something no matter how hard it is, like sticking with math for years and years and not quitting when it gets difficult. I have proven this by a major reading day I did recently. I read for 7 hrs 45 min throughout the day. I read 335 pages and ended up finishing the book and on top of that I also did 1 hour of deep math. That right there is GRIT. I was very very tired. I wanted to quit at times but I didn't. I kept going. I felt drained and out of energy at times but that didn't stop me from achieving my goal of finishing the book. So I think I have this.

15. Resilience. It is very important. It means that even when something comes and hits your life and makes it suck you keep on going after some time anyways. I can prove this by 1 example. So I was in gym class playing stormy seas. I ran and I fell. I ended up breaking 2 of my main bones, having to have surgery, staying in the hospital for 2-3 whole nights and suffering through miserable pain for the first 5 days. But that didn't stop me 1-2 weeks after I started my studying AGAIN. That's what tells me that I have resilience.

16. Reading. This is one of the most important skills you could have at an early age. Reading literally improves creativity, makes you imaginative, understand the world and people better, and it helps you improve vocabulary in a fun way. I can do this by reading good, interesting, fun books (I already do this, but can spend more time and need more consistency with reading, something I can get better at).

17. Cooking. This is also an EXTREMELY important skill for later. You need to be able to cook, in case you have to cook for yourself when you grow up, or anyone else. Cooking can teach you a lot of things by mixing ingredients. I don't necessarily have to do this right now since I am still only 12 yrs old, but I can do it if I ever want to do it for fun.

18. Organization. You need to be able to organize stuff because later in life you need to stay organized and clean. Learning how to organize stuff and keeping everything clean leaves a really big impact on how you act later in life. I think I am not that good at this, but I can be better at it by cleaning after I make a mess, folding my clothes and lots more.

19. Sleeping on time. This is a boring, but very important one. This one literally decides how you feel in the day, how your mood is, whether you're cranky or not and lots more. Without sleep our body can not function properly. I feel like even though I don't enjoy doing this one I sleep on time. On weekdays I go to bed around 8-9:30 and about the same time on weekends.

20. One last one is to focus on school. People underestimate this one, but if you don't pay attention in class you don't understand anything and don't do well on tests. I think I am good at this. I just get a bit distracted sometimes and that is normal. But I find when I don't pay attention in class I don't understand stuff. So that's why it is so important.

These are 20 skills that I think will matter the most in the future. These are 20 skills that literally predict and change how you think, feel, communicate and what type of person you become in the future. These 20 skills not only will make you extremely strong as a human in all areas but will also keep you prepared for the future.
